pages.full_text:
In the afternoon Charles, William + Peter did homework + Chris wrote letters while Simon + John went for another walk.

[photo glued in: stone village street with flowers and rooftops]

[illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible]
pages.notes: Page contains text at the top (continuation from previous page) and a large glued-in colour photo of a stone village street scene with flowers. Below the photo there is faint, partially obscured text that is very difficult to read — it may be text showing through from behind the photo or from the reverse of the glued element; transcribed as illegible. No handwritten page number visible at top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
The sky was clear & the night cold.
__Sun 26th Sept__

    It was cold & drizzly in the morning. We went to Naunton church which was friendly, [?]&[?] [?vagram] but full of old people. 1662 Communion with sentences, collect & gospell from ASB. Beautifully kept church & good organist. Stone inscription next to the alter to [?]Oscar's son? "barbarously murdered by rebels in 1645". A list of people who came back in safety from Great War.
    We had coffee & walked around the back roads of the village.
    We drove to __Bourton-on-the-Water__ & couldn't find the hotel we stayed in. We all enjoyed the model village. We wandered around the village & had fish & chips with delicious chips. Then an ice cream.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 31 at top right. 'Sun 26th Sept' is underlined. The word between 'friendly' and 'but' is unclear — possibly a superscript annotation or correction; transcribed as [?vagram]. 'gospell' is the diary's spelling. 'ASB' likely refers to Alternative Service Book. The phrase about 'barbarously murdered by rebels in 1645' is a quoted inscription. The name before 'son' is unclear — could be 'Oscar' or another name. September 26 was a Sunday in 1993 and 1999; without clearer year evidence from the page itself, 1993 is used as the iso_guess (volume A covers 1989–2003 and mid-volume position loosely suggests early-to-mid 1990s), but confidence is low. Bourton-on-the-Water is underlined in the diary.
